# uptimekuma_notification_teltonika (Resource)

Teltonika notification resource. Uses a Teltonika RUTxxx series router as an SMS gateway over its HTTP API. This provider is only compatible with Teltonika RutOS >= 7.14.0 devices.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Teltonika notification using the router's default modem
resource "uptimekuma_notification_teltonika" "sms" {
  name         = "Teltonika SMS"
  url          = "https://192.168.1.1"
  username     = "admin"
  password     = "your-router-password"
  phone_number = "+1234567890"
  is_active    = true
  is_default   = false
}

# Teltonika notification with a specific modem and a self-signed certificate
resource "uptimekuma_notification_teltonika" "self_signed" {
  name         = "Teltonika SMS (self-signed)"
  url          = "https://teltonika.example.com:8080"
  username     = "admin"
  password     = "your-router-password"
  modem        = "1-1"
  phone_number = "+1234567890,+1234567891"
  unsafe_tls   = true
  is_active    = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Notification name
- `password` (String, Sensitive) The password for the router user.
- `phone_number` (String) The recipient phone number (e.g., "+336xxxxxxxx"). Multiple recipients can be provided as a comma-separated list.
- `url` (String) Teltonika router base URL (e.g., https://192.168.1.1).
- `username` (String) The router user used to authenticate against the Teltonika HTTP API.

### Optional

- `apply_existing` (Boolean)
- `is_active` (Boolean)
- `is_default` (Boolean)
- `modem` (String) The modem identifier on the router (e.g., "1-1").
- `unsafe_tls` (Boolean) Disables TLS certificate validation when communicating with the router. This is useful for routers using a self-signed certificate.

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) Notification identifier
